ocr.text: The table presents a detailed overview of genetic alterations associated with the THBS1 gene, located at chromosome 15q14,
specifically focusing on the variant identified by the ID LN607833. This variant is characterized by the presence of ten skipped
exons, which may significantly impact the gene's functional output. Skipped exons can lead to alterations in the open reading
frame (ORF) and may affect the resulting protein's structure and function. The ORF change associated with this variant
indicates a potential disruption in the normal coding sequence, which may result in a truncated or nonfunctional protein.
Gene Name  Positions  ID  Skipped Exons  ORF Change  Affected Domain(s)  Protein Length
THBS1  15q14  LN607833  10  Del  TSP1  1112
LGALS1  22q13.1  LN607840  3  Frameshift  β-galactoside binding  39
RAP1GAP  1p36.12  LN607839  24  Ins  N/A  821
CD47  3q13.12  LN680437  8–10  Frameshift  Cytoplasmic C-term  293
RHOA  3p21.31  LN607834  3–4  Del  GTP binding  109
RHOD  11q13.2  LN607835  4  Del  GTP binding  165
CASK  Xp11.4  LN607837  19–20  Del  Linker between PDZ and SH3  886
CTTN  11q13.3  LN607836  10–11  Del  Cortactin  476
SEMA3C  7q21.11  LN626689  15  Frameshift  Type 1 Ig-like and R/K rich  514
SEMA3F  3p21.31  LN626688  16  Frameshift  Type 1 Ig-like and R/K rich  571
PLXNB1  3p21.31  LN626690  32  Frameshift  Cytoplasmic C-term  1876
Furthermore, the affected domains listed in the table provide insights into specific functional regions within the protein that may
be compromised due to these genetic alterations. Understanding the implications of such changes is crucial for elucidating the
role of THBS1 in various biological processes and its potential involvement in disease mechanisms. The protein length metric
adds context to the overall impact of the skipped exons, as alterations in protein size can influence its stability, localization, and
interaction with other cellular components. Thus, this data serves as a foundation for further investigation into the biological
significance of THBS1 variants.
